summary:
Claim: In alpha-thalassemia trait, HbF and HbA2 levels are normal on electrophoresis.  
Evidence: - Alpha-thalassemia affects only alpha-globin chain synthesis. - Gamma and beta globin chains (responsible for HbF and HbA2) are unaffected.  
Answer: B

Claim: In alpha-thalassemia trait, HbF and HbA2 levels are normal on electrophoresis.  
Evidence: - Alpha-thalassemia specifically disrupts alpha-globin chain production without altering gamma or beta-globin synthesis. - As a result, hemoglobin variants such as HbF and HbA2 remain within typical reference ranges on electrophoresis.  
Answer: B
